metadata.description: Sulis AI plugin marketplace — methodology studios, requirements analysis, facilitation, context cartography, right-sized architecture, disciplined change primitives, deterministic code intelligence, founder coordination, and autonomous Work Package execution end-to-end. v1.19.0 adds sulis-business-strategy (Kind-schema variant of the business-strategy studio, v0.1.0 test fixture): ships the Identity Kind — a four-stage Kind manifest (find → generate → evaluate → decide) that replaces the legacy identity-articulation outcome's three-lens triad with a single evaluate stage + rubric + structured Verdict + reason-class routing. The triad's function (multi-perspective evaluation with consensus) is preserved structurally in evaluate + decide; its mechanism (three parallel agent lanes) is replaced. Coexists with the established sulis-strategy plugin. Until the platform engine wires Slices 2-4 of the Kind compiler, the plugin's business-strategist agent drives all four stages manually; when the engine catches up, the agent simplifies to invoking the Kind via KindHandler with no Kind YAML change. Test fixture for promoting the migration pattern to studios. v1.16.0 publishes the Repository Contract Standard (RC-01..RC-13) at plugins/srd/references/repository-contract-standard.md — the marketplace-wide specification of the GitHub repository shape every Sulis agent assumes. Locks: dev/main two-branch model, branch protections, GitHub Merge Queue config on dev (group size 5, concurrency 3, failure tolerance 3 per the Shopify pattern), six required Actions workflows (branch-ci on pull_request, merge-queue-ci on merge_group, deploy-staging on push to dev, health-and-smoke on workflow_run, promote-dev-to-main on workflow_dispatch, release-prod on push to main), staging + production environments, per-env deploy tokens, squash-only repo settings, v* SemVer tag protection, token scopes, and the agent arrival check (executor Step 0) with structured JSON output. Strict mode for v1: agents refuse non-conforming repos and surface a delta list in founder English. Composes with GIT-01..GIT-10 (operationalises the two-branch model on GitHub), EL-01..EL-08 (adds Step 0 to the executor lifecycle), and the cicd-batching-analysis.md research (the merge-queue config encodes its per-batch deploy recommendation). Companion scripts wpx-arrival-check + wpx-bootstrap-repo specified in RC-11 and the Bootstrap Workflow section; implementation scheduled for the next sulis-execution release. The cicd-batching-analysis.md technical research ships alongside at plugins/sulis-execution/docs/research/ documenting the merge-queue convention (Bors → Shopify Shipit → GitHub Merge Queue) and SEA's natural batch unit (DAG topological levels). All v1.15.x capabilities preserved.

plugins.name: srd
plugins.description: Requirements Analyst — facilitates building Software Requirements Documents backed by UML artifacts through guided conversation. Includes intent triage, disambiguation sweep, and adversarial sweep phases; reads .context/{project}/INDEX.md from sulis-context to apply Respect-Don't-Restate; hands architecture work over to SEA. Publishes marketplace-wide standards used by every plugin: Founder English (FE-01..FE-11), the Repository Contract Standard (RC-01..RC-13), the PR Hygiene Standard (PH-01..PH-08), Engineering Principles, Git Workflow, Convention Preference, Audience-Adapted Framing, Executor Loop, and Red-Green-Blue. v1.20.0 shipped Repository Contract Standard. v1.21.0: publishes the PR Hygiene Standard at plugins/srd/references/pr-hygiene-standard.md (PH-01..PH-08). The marketplace-wide specification of change-artifact shape — Scope (one logical concern per PR), Size (reviewable in one sitting; Google's 200/400 line thresholds as calibration starting points), Safety (high-risk surfaces — migrations, schemas, secrets, infra config, lock-file dominance — flagged regardless of total size), Completeness (code + tests + docs ship together). Honest synthesis of Google's eng-practices, SmartBear/Cisco 2006 review study (recency-capped), DORA change-failure-rate research, Squawk, Strong Migrations, Fowler's Expand-Contract, Conventional Commits, and Trunk-Based Development doctrine. Pre-flight Critical Thinking Standard analysis passed: MECE (pairwise mutual-exclusivity verified, leg-test passed, so-what test passed); Primitive Grounding (irreducible at PR-review level); Balanced Investigation (counter-evidence section discloses Facebook big-diff practice + SmartBear recency caveat + splitting-theatre risk); Adversarial Testing (three rejected alternatives documented — extend MECE-3, fold into Quality lens, distribute across existing standards); Falsifiability (stop/pivot/re-evaluate criteria + pre-mortem); Confidence Calibration (each primitive SUPPORTED, synthesis UNVALIDATED until calibration data accrues). 90-day calibration window from 2026-05-21. Anchor case PR-168. Consumed by /code-review via SEA v0.14.0+ (CR-09); designed for extension to any future PR-touching skill via the PH-06 signal-table contract.

plugins.name: sulis-context
plugins.description: Context Cartographer — discovers existing architecture documentation, ADRs, conventions, and standards in a project; produces .context/{project}/INDEX.md so downstream plugins (SRD, SEA, sulis-security) respect prior decisions instead of restating them. Folder-structure-agnostic; works on any project shape.

plugins.name: sulis-strategy
plugins.description: Business strategy studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides vision, strategy, principles, commercial, GTM, and roadmap skills for strategic foundation work. v1.3.2: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). Lead with outcomes; concrete over abstract; no mechanism narration. v1.3.3: adds FE-11 (Inference Over Interrogation) citation — infer from existing context before asking the founder; never relay a specialist's 'open questions' verbatim.

plugins.name: sulis-business-strategy
plugins.description: Business Strategy studio (Kind-schema variant). Drives business-context intake, identity, brand positioning, voice, principles, and strategic foundation work through Kind-schema workflows. v0.1.0 ships TWO Kinds wired end-to-end: BusinessContext (entry point — 9 domains × 34 questions of conversational intake with V/A/U scoring and SHA-256 provenance, producing BUSINESS_CONTEXT.md; replaces the legacy business-context-intake outcome's Domain Expert / Assumption Challenger / Completeness Monitor triad; three session modes — greenfield / baseline / progressive — auto-detect from workspace state) and Identity (first consumer — articulates organisational identity via Golden Circle WHY→HOW→WHAT, producing IDENTITY.md; replaces the legacy identity-articulation outcome's Belief Crystallizer / Authenticity Validator / Expression Architect triad; pre-checks BUSINESS_CONTEXT.md exists via spec.find.prerequisite_inputs and halts cleanly if missing — no silent degradation). The two-Kind sequence tests both the schema's stage primitives AND the cross-Kind dependency pattern (BusinessContext produces BUSINESS_CONTEXT.md, Identity declares it as required, every future Kind follows the same pattern). Each Kind replaces its legacy triad with a single evaluate stage + rubric + structured Verdict + reason-class routing (content_deficient → regenerate, context_deficient → re-find, technical_transient → retry, technical_fatal → stop). The BusinessContext rubric covers coverage (≥70% question coverage, all 9 domains addressed), defensibility (every Validated has provenance, every Assumed has rationale + validation path), domain coherence (no contradictions, no drift from existing artifacts), boundary compliance (B1 blocks strategic prescription — context captures WHAT not what-to-do). The Identity rubric covers authenticity / falsifiability / distinctiveness / voice / evidence quality. Until the platform engine wires Slices 2-4 of the Kind compiler, the business-strategist agent drives all four stages manually; when the engine catches up, the agent simplifies to invoking each Kind via KindHandler with no Kind YAML change. The plugin includes two four-stage Kind manifests (references/kinds/BusinessContext.yaml + Identity.yaml), two rubrics, two instance schemas, and two skills (:context entry point + :identity consumer). Sibling Kinds (Brand, ToneOfVoice, Principles, Vision, Strategy, Commercial, GTM) follow. Coexists with the established sulis-strategy plugin (legacy triad model); both can be installed simultaneously.

plugins.name: sulis-product-development
plugins.description: Product development studio for the Outcome-First Methodology. Provides design, plan, implement, and complete skills for the full product delivery lifecycle. v1.2.2: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). plugins.name: sulis-platform-sdk
plugins.description: Platform SDK — build production-ready SaaS backends with built-in auth, billing, multi-tenancy, and observability

plugins.name: sea
plugins.description: Senior Engineering Architect — designs hardened architectures, audits brownfield code for primitive gaps, and decomposes designs into atomic Work Packages with Red-Green-Blue verification. /sea:probe v0.9.0 uses a Python orchestrator (scripts/probe.py) to invoke ast-grep + lizard + scc + git plus optional tools (jscpd, ts-prune, vulture, deadcode, dependency-cruiser, import-linter, detect-secrets) across 17 deterministic phases — 15 per-workspace + 2 repo-wide (Deployment Topology with first-class Sulis manifest support, Credential Scanning hash-only / baseline-aware). Polyglot 4-stage workspace enumeration adds auxiliary packages, code-bearing dirs, and deployment-only dirs alongside the existing monorepo manifests. Outputs structured JSON + Markdown + a self-contained navigable HTML report. 88+ unit + integration tests; no LLM fallback. Every WP carries a change primitive (22-primitive Minto-pyramid catalogue). v0.11.6: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). Lead with outcomes; concrete over abstract; no mechanism narration. v0.11.7: adds FE-11 (Inference Over Interrogation) citation — infer from existing context before asking the founder; never relay a specialist's 'open questions' verbatim. v0.12.0: adds /code-review skill — a PR-scoped umbrella that runs three lenses in parallel (architecture / security / general quality) against a PR number, branch ref, or commit range. Scans the diff plus a one-hop neighbour ring (callers, callees, shared-import modules) capped at 20 files; findings in the diff can block merge, findings in the neighbour ring are surfaced as exposure (downgraded one severity, never merge-blocking). Invokes /sulis-security:codebase-assess in Quick mode for the security lens. Outputs a single merged report at .architecture/{project}/code-reviews/PR-{number}-{date}.md plus draft Hardening Deltas with source: code-review:PR-NNN. Advisory only — never posts to the PR, never sets status checks, never auto-blocks. v0.13.0: publishes the Code Review Standard at plugins/sea/references/code-review-standard.md (CR-01..CR-08). Closes BUG_REPORT.md — a production session against honestmobile/honest-smart-sim-platform#168 showed /code-review v0.12.0 missing a build-breaking TypeScript error that a free claude[bot] caught in seconds via tsc --noEmit. The standard makes mechanical floors mandatory: CR-01 typecheck + lint on BASE and HEAD before any lens runs; CR-02 parallel-dispatch mandatory above 200 lines / 5 files; CR-03 full-file reads on changed files >50 lines (sampling forbidden); CR-04 evidence discipline (file:line + quoted text); CR-05 severity rubric with objective conditions; CR-06 verdict computed not chosen with three auto-downgrade rules the agent cannot override (Build Verification non-empty → minimum Block; unread file → minimum Request changes; silent lens → minimum Request changes); CR-07 lens completion criteria (Quality lens must produce six structured outputs including the JSX-identifier scan that catches the PR-168 class of bug); CR-08 self-attestation in Methodology section. /code-review skill rewritten to implement CR-01..CR-08 by reference rather than restating them. Anchor case PR-168 documented in the standard for calibration tracking. v0.14.0: adds CR-09 (PR Hygiene Application) to the Code Review Standard and rewires /code-review to apply the new PR Hygiene Standard (PH-01..PH-08) published by SRD v1.21.0. Closes user feedback that 'number of migration files present in the PR should have been flagged' was a missing indicator. The hygiene check runs before lens dispatch, computes a deterministic signal table (diff size, file count, commit-type spread, module fan-out, migration count, schema/IDL count, infra-file count, lock-file ratio, secret-pattern hits, new-source-without-test count), and feeds CR-06 verdict auto-downgrade rule 4 — PH-03 high findings (4+ migrations, plaintext secret pattern) → minimum verdict Request changes. Hygiene findings appear in a dedicated section of the report between Build Verification and lens findings; they are author recommendations (split, add tests, review migration order) and do NOT produce Hardening Deltas (lens findings remain the source of deltas). Designed for extension to any future PR-touching skill via the PH-06 signal table contract. v0.15.0: rewrites /code-review report format to a two-tier shape per user feedback that the report is read by non-technical founders (often using AI to generate code) and currently fails FE-06 — internal IDs, engineer jargon, and raw severity labels make the report unreadable to its primary audience. Tier 1 (top of report) is founder-facing: plain English sections (At a glance / What to fix / How this pull request is shaped / Things to take away), action-oriented labels (Ready to merge / Must fix / etc.), recommendations cite codebase patterns by file path, and an optional 'Things to take away' section (max 3 lessons, tied to specific findings, anti-condescension rules — omit when the PR is clean). Tier 2 (below Technical detail heading) is unchanged for engineers and downstream agents. Chat summary shortened to 3-4 sentences since the report itself is now founder-readable.

plugins.name: sulis-security
plugins.description: Security & Viability Reviewer — runs a 25-primitive codebase viability assessment (security, data protection, code quality, supply chain, infrastructure) via an OODA-spiral methodology. Composes with SEA for hardening and with SRD for requirements-traceable findings. v0.3.2: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). Lead with outcomes; concrete over abstract; no mechanism narration. v0.3.3: adds FE-11 (Inference Over Interrogation) citation — infer from existing context before asking the founder; never relay a specialist's 'open questions' verbatim.

plugins.name: idc
plugins.description: Investor Deck Coach — facilitates Sequoia-style pitch deck creation through guided conversation. Stage-aware (angel, pre-seed, seed, Series A, Series B). v0.4.0: aligns paths with the production convention proven on the sulis pre-seed deck — numbered phase folders (02-research/, 03-financials/, 04-narrative/, 05-adversarial/, 06-verification/), multi-file journal/, ADVERSARIAL_REVIEW.md, VERIFICATION_REPORT.md. Adds three investor-facing HTML deliverables at .pitch root: PITCH.html (long-form scrollable web pitch for DocSend-style sharing), FINANCIALS.html (investor-facing financial summary), REVIEW.html (investor-facing adversarial-review summary). Three new build scripts: build_web_pitch.py, build_investor_financials.py, build_review_html.py. Cites FE-01..FE-11 (Founder English including Inference Over Interrogation) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md.

plugins.name: sulis-execution
plugins.description: Work Package Executor — three-layer architecture (per v0.9.0) plus plan-mode-style Step 1.5 plan generation (v0.10.0+). v0.10.6 fixes a false-positive in _detect_branch_ci: the v0.10.4 substring-grep heuristic matched any Conventional Commits prefix anywhere in a workflow YAML — including paths-ignore filters. A real deploy-on-push-to-dev workflow with `paths-ignore: ['docs/**']` was misclassified as branch CI (because `docs/` matched the substring), causing wpx-pipeline to poll for 45 min waiting for check-runs that never appeared. Replaced with a structural parser that walks each .github/workflows/*.y[a]ml file looking specifically for `branches:` declarations (under on.push or on.pull_request), parsing inline lists and block lists. Explicitly rejects `branches-ignore`, `paths`, `paths-ignore`, `tags`, and comments. Two new regression tests pinned: paths-ignore must not register; branches-ignore must not register. v0.10.5 shipped the pytest test suite + two bug fixes (already-merged branch detection + multi-table INDEX parsing). v0.10.4 fixed emit_ok exit_code + no-branch-CI hang. v0.10.3 closed the auto-draft INDEX-registration gap. v0.10.2 fixed wpx-step12 dispatcher correctness. v0.10.1 fixed wpx-* tool discoverability. 43 tests across unit/ + integration/. Composes with CP-01..CP-05, AAF-01..AAF-09, EP-02/03/07, RGB-01..03. v0.10.7: wpx-pipeline's Step 10a health check now auto-detects the health path from --smoke-cmd's URL (e.g. `curl -sf https://staging/health` → /health), with --health-path flag for explicit override. Fixes the bug where APIs whose root returns 404 by design (health only at /health) produced spurious 10-minute hangs followed by health_status: unhealthy BLOCKERs. Backward-compatible: falls back to / when no URL is in the smoke command. Three regression tests pinned (49 tests total).

plugins.name: sulis-concierge
plugins.description: Founder Concierge — the single entry point for non-technical founders building a product through the Sulis AI marketplace. Orchestrates the journey from idea → requirements → design → implementation → verification → security in plain English. Spawns specialist agents (SRD, SEA, sulis-security, sulis-context, sulis-execution) at the right time, reads their produced artifacts, translates technical output into founder English, maintains journey state across sessions in .concierge/{project}/JOURNEY.md. Applies AAF-01..AAF-09 aggressively at every founder-facing message. Start a session with `claude --agent sulis-concierge`. v0.1.6: cites Founder English (FE-01..FE-10) at plugins/srd/references/founder-english.md — every founder-facing chat message AND founder-readable artifact write passes the FE-06 five-point check (strip internal IDs, translate filenames, expand acronyms, strip internal taxonomy, read-aloud test). Lead with outcomes; concrete over abstract; no mechanism narration. v0.1.7: deep restructure adopting patterns from tria's explorer.yaml + production-guardian.md + retired concierge.yaml + strategy.yaml. Adds: 'you think this internally / you say this externally' framing; a five-phase pre-emission mechanical gate (Load → Categorise → Triage → Decide → Emit); 5-lens analysis for specialist output (gap-matching / strategy-divergence / journey-alignment / feature-implications / opportunity-detection); the 'What I heard / What I noticed / What I recommend' synthesis template; explicit three-state output model (PROCEED / GATHER / BLOCKED) with enumerated forbidden output shapes (three-questions, specialist-output tables, mechanism narration, menus of next steps, permission-theatre closures). Closes the relay-and-translate failure that v1.15.0's FE block didn't catch (anchor case 3 in founder-english.md). v0.1.8: closes the wording-variant gap from v1.15.2 partial fix. Expands the permission-theatre wording list with pattern-match anchors. Adds Recommendation = Action MUST rule: when you make a recommendation, you act on it in the same message; the closing line is action-then-report, never a permission question. v0.1.9: extends the Pre-Emission Gate explicitly to mid-task status updates AND Agent-tool `description` parameters — they are founder-facing chrome and pass the same FE-06 scan as chat messages. Adds BAD/GOOD pairs covering background-task announcements, Agent-tool descriptions, and pipeline status. Closes the v1.15.3 partial-fix where chat messages translated but the harness chrome did not.
